svg.text: TIGRINYA
GE'EZ / ETHIOPIC SCRIPT
GE'EZ SCRIPT — WORLD'S OLDEST ALPHABETS
Ge'ez (Ethiopic) script: derived from ancient South Arabian
~4th c. CE: adopted for ancient Ethiopian language Ge'ez
Left-to-right abugida: each symbol = consonant + vowel combination
7 vowel orders per consonant = 7 forms of each letter
TIGRINYA IN GE'EZ SCRIPT
First order (inherent /ə/): ሀ ለ መ ረ ሰ ቀ በ ተ ቸ ነ
Second order (/u/): ሁ ሉ ሙ ሩ ሱ ቁ ቡ ቱ ቹ ኑ
Third order (/i/): ሂ ሊ ሚ ሪ ሲ ቂ ቢ ቲ ቺ ኒ
245 total syllabic characters in standard Tigrinya set
SPECIAL TIGRINYA LETTERS
Pharyngeal consonants: ዐ /ʕa/ ዑ /ʕu/ ... (voiced pharyngeal)
Ejective consonants: ጸ /pʼ/ ጸ ጸ (unique to Ethiopic languages)
No punctuation system in classical Ge'ez (four dots = full stop)
OROMO · AFAAN OROMOO · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
QUBEE — OROMO'S LATIN ALPHABET
Oromo uses Latin script (Qubee) adopted 1991 after Derg falls
Political script choice: rejected Ethiopic/Ge'ez as Amhara symbol
Long vowels: doubled letters aa ii uu ee oo
Pharyngeals in Qubee: x = /ħ/ '(apostrophe) for glottals
SCRIPT CONTRAST: MAXIMUM DIVERGENCE
Tigrinya: Ge'ez syllabary (245 characters)
Oromo: Latin alphabet (26 + doubled vowels)
Both in Ethiopia: Oromo rejected Ge'ez as political statement
Eritrea: Ge'ez used for Tigrinya • Ethiopia: Latin for Oromo
SHARED HORN OF AFRICA CONTEXT
Tigrinya speakers: Eritrea + Tigray (northern Ethiopia)
Oromo speakers: central/southern Ethiopia + northern Kenya
Addis Ababa sits in Oromo territory; Tigrinya ~200mi north
Two writing systems; one Horn of Africa history
PLATE 34a ALT · TIGRINYA / OROMO · SCRIPT
